subject: Project Management
title: Philip Tetlock's Superforecasters
description: A 60-second lesson on Philip Tetlock's Superforecasters.

hook.text: Philip Tetlock ran a forecasting tournament. Ordinary people beat CIA analysts with one key skill.

buildup.text: These 'superforecasters' weren't smarter. They were better at updating their beliefs with new evidence.

discovery.text: Superforecasters think in probabilities, not certainties. They say '70% likely' instead of 'it will happen.'

twist.text: The trait that predicted forecasting ability wasn't IQ — it was intellectual humility.

climax.text: Tetlock showed that good forecasting is a learnable skill — not a talent reserved for experts.

punchline.text: The best forecasters are humble, not brilliant.

quiz.question: What skill did Tetlock's superforecasters share?
options: Updating their beliefs with new evidence and thinking in probabilities
